header_summary:
Recent discussions within the Bitcoin development community have underscored the importance of consensus and collective action in maintaining the network's security, particularly in the face of potential threats from quantum computing. Innovations like the Zero-Knowledge quantum-safe spending option and the Hourglass V2 proposal for block rewards, based on empirical on-chain data, represent significant strides towards enhancing Bitcoin's resilience and governance mechanisms. [More about Bitcoin's governance and security enhancements](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CADL_X_eb8byZs3VKORoUzaPoBvguSbxJ=KyKifsQFWFqyqHeRA@mail.gmail.com/T/#meb39db4157bc9d1644f0821a0ded01c45b).

The release of Bitcoin Inquisition 29.2 and Bitcoin Core 29.3 has introduced critical updates aimed at improving the system's robustness and user experience. While the former focuses on Consensus Cleanup through a bugfix soft fork, the latter brings performance enhancements, bug fixes, and UX improvements, notably advocating the significance of a graphical user interface for fostering wider adoption and ensuring security. These updates, tested across various platforms, highlight the community's role in validating these improvements. [Details on Bitcoin Core's latest updates](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAO3Wf41sAHeZ-KR1mp=xNuydstcMk9R93ou5Jobmq9ngn7TKXA@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m110222ead81e7ac2f1becbfe3e4f49db3200dafc).

Furthermore, advancements in cryptographic policies such as the integration of Discrete Log Contracts (DLC) with Bitcoin Lightning Invoice Signature Key (BLISK) for conditional compilation and the exploration of Layer 2 (L2) application covenants highlight the ongoing efforts to enhance security and flexibility. These discussions reveal a keen focus on reducing reliance on interactivity and strengthening trust assumptions in transaction protocols, illustrating the community's proactive approach to addressing both existing and emerging challenges in Bitcoin's ecosystem. [Exploring cryptographic policy enhancements](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/blisk-boolean-circuit-logic-integrated-into-the-single-key/2217/15).

recent_posts.title: Hourglass V2 Update
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin's rules allow users to prevent malicious UTXO claims through collective action.
- The Hourglass V2 proposal improves on V1, but critiques its block reward assignment.
- A new BIP includes a quantum-safe spending option, enhancing security against quantum threats.

recent_posts.title: BIP 54 active on Bitcoin Inquisition
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Inquisition 29.2 now supports BIP 54, improving system robustness without new features.
- It introduces a bugfix soft fork, effective from block 291168, with limited testing opportunities.
- Community testing across Signet networks is crucial for verifying the update's effectiveness.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v29.3 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core 29.3, with enhancements and bug fixes, is now available for download.
- It supports updates from EOL versions and is tested on major operating systems.
- Significant changes include P2P network updates, wallet and mining improvements.

recent_posts.title: The future of the Bitcoin Core GUI
recent_posts.summary:
- The UX, including a GUI, is vital for Bitcoin Core's adoption and security.
- Removing the GUI could push users towards less secure, third-party options.
- A comprehensive UX/UI is necessary for Bitcoin Core's long-term viability and security.

recent_posts.title: BLISK: Boolean circuit Logic Integrated into the Single Key
recent_posts.summary:
- BLISK enhancement with DLC adaptor points allows conditional cryptographic policy relaxation.
- Adaptor points enable policy changes based on oracle-confirmed events, reducing required signatures.
- Benefits include automatic adjustments and minimal participant interaction, but oracle reliability and security pose challenges.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin PIPEs v2
recent_posts.summary:
- Layer 2 applications use scripts and musig for security and consensus.
- Trust is crucial in L2 protocols, ensured by mechanisms like HTLCs for safe transactions.
- Early 3-3 musig revelation by operators may streamline protocol processes, increasing trust.

active_posts.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal suggests Bitcoin should adapt to different signature algorithms for future security.
- A dual signature system, with a primary and emergency algorithm, is introduced for resilience.
- Supporting new wallet standards and software modifications are essential for this security upgrade.

active_posts.title: Addressing remaining points on BIP 54
active_posts.summary:
- BIP 54's debate reignites, conflicting on nLockTime's use for extranonce and ASIC optimization concerns.
- Jeremy Rubin proposes a sparse Merkle tree over restricting transaction sizes, facing skepticism.
- Consensus prefers pragmatic solutions over drastic protocol changes, aiming for Bitcoin ecosystem's stability.

active_posts.title: The limitations of cryptographic agility in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- The Bitcoin community debates cryptographic agility to enhance security and adaptability.
- Concerns arise over fungibility impact due to divergent preferences for cryptographic primitives.
- Consensus on upgrades and the transition to new standards like FancySig is challenging.

active_posts.title: Great Consensus Cleanup Revival
active_posts.summary:
- The Great Consensus Cleanup targets Bitcoin protocol flaws to enhance security and efficiency.
- It proposes adjustments and constraints to tackle vulnerabilities, like the timewarp exploit.
- Community debate emerges over proposals affecting scalability and protocol functionality.

active_posts.title: SuperScalar: Laddered Timeout-Tree-Structured Decker-Wattenhofer Factories
active_posts.summary:
- The SuperScalar mechanism enhances Bitcoin Lightning Network's liquidity without blockchain changes.
- It employs Decker-Wattenhofer mechanisms, timeout trees, and laddering strategies for scalability.
- Practical implementation includes incentivizing user activity and optimizing tree structures for efficiency.

active_posts.title: Major BIP 360 Update
active_posts.summary:
- Ethan Heilman, Isabel Foxen Duke, and Hunter Beast rewrote BIP 360 for clarity.
- BIP 360 introduces "Pay-to-Tapscript-Hash" to combat quantum threats, omitting key path spend.
- The update adds Python and Rust test vectors, inviting community review for quantum resistance.

today_in_history_posts.title: SIGHASH_NOINPUT in Segregated Witness
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Segwit, aiming to resolve transaction malleability, may incorporate a new SIGHASH flag.
- This flag, SIGHASH_NOINPUT, enables cheaper third-party blockchain monitoring by not signing the input txid/index.
- If successful, a draft BIP for this solution will be proposed soon.

today_in_history_posts.title: SIGHASH_NOINPUT in Segregated Witness
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Joseph Poon introduced a SIGHASH_NOINPUT flag excluding outpoint and amount in signatures.
- He provided links to proposed SIGHASH types and a customizable nHashType draft.
- Concerns about OP_CODESEPARATOR and replay attacks with SIGHASH_NOINPUT were addressed, including a GitHub draft.

today_in_history_posts.title: An Onchain Implementation Of Mining Feerate Futures
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Mining fee futures aim to stabilize unpredictable blockchain fees for users and miners.
- A binary contract allows trading on fee predictions, using Taproot for transaction execution.
- The scheme protects against fee volatility, ensuring efficiency and fairness in blockchain space usage.
